title: Xochiquetzallia mortoniana
intro: Xochiquetzallia mortoniana, also known as Mexican prickly poppy, is a flowering plant native to Mexico and the southwestern United States, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and edible seeds. The health benefits of Xochiquetzallia mortoniana include its potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate pain, and act as an antioxidant, making it a valuable addition to natural remedies. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Xochiquetzallia mortoniana is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, colds, and digestive issues.
